basic.name: Critical thinking
basic.dailyDrill: Read one argument online. Write 3 counterpoints to it in 2 minutes. No googling.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: spot one logical fallacy in the wild and name it precisely.

basic.name: Analytical thinking
basic.dailyDrill: Break one problem you have today into its smallest components. List every part.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: produce one structured analysis with data, not just opinion.

basic.name: Decision-making
basic.dailyDrill: Name one decision you've been avoiding. Write the two options and their consequences. Pick one.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: make 5 decisions you would have previously postponed.

basic.name: Working (short-term) memory
basic.dailyDrill: Memorize 10 random digits. Close eyes. Recall. Repeat twice.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: hold a 5-step process in memory without writing it down.

basic.name: Long-term memory retention
basic.dailyDrill: Recall one concept you learned 7+ days ago. Write a 3-sentence explanation from memory only.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: teach one concept from 2 weeks ago to an imaginary student.

basic.name: Attention span & cognitive control
basic.dailyDrill: Do one 25-minute Pomodoro — phone face down, no tabs. Log the exact minute you first felt the urge to switch.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: complete 5 uninterrupted Pomodoros. Track your distraction time.

basic.name: Executive function
basic.dailyDrill: Write your 3 tasks for tomorrow in priority order. Assign a specific time block to each. No vague entries.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: complete your pre-planned tasks 4 out of 5 days.

basic.name: Creativity & originality
basic.dailyDrill: Take one thing you built today. Think of 3 wildly different uses or extensions for it. No filtering.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: generate one genuinely novel idea per day. Write them all down.

basic.name: Problem-solving
basic.dailyDrill: Pick one bug or blocker. Write the problem statement in one precise sentence before touching anything.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: solve 3 problems by writing the problem statement first, every time.

basic.name: Metacognitive regulation
basic.dailyDrill: After today's study block: rate your understanding 1–10. Write what specifically makes it not a 10.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: identify one consistent gap in how you learn. Write your fix.

basic.name: Self-regulated learning skills
basic.dailyDrill: Name one study method that didn't work today. Write the replacement strategy for tomorrow.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: run 2 intentional learning experiments. Document results.

basic.name: Digital literacy & source evaluation
basic.dailyDrill: Fact-check one claim you read today. Write the primary source you used — not Wikipedia.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: evaluate 5 sources for credibility using author + evidence + bias check.

basic.name: Cognitive flexibility
basic.dailyDrill: Take one approach to a problem today. Stop. Solve it a completely different way.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: hold two contradictory ideas simultaneously and argue both sides.

basic.name: Communication
basic.dailyDrill: Rewrite one technical explanation you wrote today in plain English — no jargon, no abbreviations.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: explain one complex concept to a non-technical person. They must nod and say they get it.

basic.name: Active listening
basic.dailyDrill: In your next conversation: ask one clarifying question before giving your response. No interrupting.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: summarize back what someone told you 3 times — make them say 'yes, exactly'.

basic.name: Clear articulation
basic.dailyDrill: Record yourself explaining today's main concept in 60 seconds. Listen back. Re-record once.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: give 3 verbal explanations without using 'um', 'like', or 'basically'.

basic.name: Body-language alignment
basic.dailyDrill: In one interaction today: check your posture mid-conversation. Adjust to open, upright, relaxed.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: notice your body language in 5 conversations. Log one adjustment each time.

basic.name: Eye contact
basic.dailyDrill: In your next conversation: hold eye contact for each complete sentence you speak.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: maintain natural eye contact in 5 conversations without breaking for distraction.

basic.name: Assertive handshake
basic.dailyDrill: Practice your introduction — name, context, handshake — in a mirror. Once. Make it feel natural.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: have one introduction where your name, role, and handshake land with confidence.

basic.name: Leadership
basic.dailyDrill: Identify one decision someone else should own. Tell them clearly, with context, to own it.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: delegate 3 things you were holding unnecessarily.

basic.name: Vision-setting
basic.dailyDrill: Write a one-paragraph vision for where you want to be in 6 months. Be specific. Dates. Outcomes.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: share your vision with one person. Get their honest reaction.

basic.name: Delegation & empowerment
basic.dailyDrill: Write one task you're holding that someone else could do. Write their name next to it.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: successfully hand off 2 tasks and follow up on them — not micromanage them.

basic.name: Accountability enforcement
basic.dailyDrill: Name one commitment you made this week. Did you keep it? Write the honest answer.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: keep every commitment you make. Make fewer, bigger ones.

basic.name: Conflict resolution
basic.dailyDrill: Name one unresolved tension. Write the ideal outcome — not who was right or wrong.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: address one conflict directly. Write what you said and what shifted.

basic.name: Inspiring followership
basic.dailyDrill: Write one thing you did today that someone else could learn from. Would you share it?
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: share one lesson with someone who could use it. No self-promotion.

basic.name: Emotional Intelligence
basic.dailyDrill: Name three emotions you felt today. Write what triggered each one. No vague answers.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: correctly identify your emotional state before reacting in 5 situations.

basic.name: Self-awareness
basic.dailyDrill: Write one blind spot you suspect you have. Write how you'd prove or disprove it this week.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: get feedback from one person on one specific behavior you want to understand.

basic.name: Self-regulation
basic.dailyDrill: Name one impulse you had today — to quit, skip, snap, or react. Write how you handled it.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: catch yourself before reacting impulsively 3 times. Log each one.

basic.name: Rapport building
basic.dailyDrill: In one interaction today: find one genuine thing in common. Mention it — don't force it.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: build one new genuine connection with someone in your field.

basic.name: Emotional resilience
basic.dailyDrill: Name one thing that went wrong today. Write one thing it taught you. No complaining.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: recover from 3 setbacks without venting or spiraling.

basic.name: Empathy & social cognition
basic.dailyDrill: Reflect back what someone said before responding — in their words, not yours.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: practice perspective-taking in 3 conversations. Write what you noticed.

basic.name: Moral reasoning & ethical decision-making
basic.dailyDrill: Name one ethical tradeoff you encountered today in tech or life. Write your position in 2 sentences.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: identify one value you hold that shapes your technical decisions.

basic.name: Emotional regulation & self-esteem
basic.dailyDrill: Notice one moment of frustration today. Name the emotion and the exact trigger. Don't suppress it.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: write 3 things you're genuinely good at. No false modesty.

basic.name: Prospective memory & time management
basic.dailyDrill: Set one specific reminder for a task 3+ days from now. Write why it matters when you set it.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: complete 5 future-you commitments you set in advance.

basic.name: Problem identification
basic.dailyDrill: Name a recurring problem in your work. Write the root cause in one sentence — not the symptom.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: identify the root cause of 3 recurring issues using 5-Whys.

basic.name: Logical reasoning
basic.dailyDrill: Make one argument today using only if/then logic. No emotional language. No filler.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: spot one logical fallacy per day in something you read.

basic.name: Bias detection
basic.dailyDrill: Name one assumption you made today that you didn't verify. Write what you assumed.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: catch yourself making 5 unverified assumptions. Write them all.

basic.name: Question framing
basic.dailyDrill: Reframe one problem you have as a 'How might we...' question. Notice how the solutions change.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: reframe 5 problems into questions. Share one with someone.

basic.name: Scenario analysis
basic.dailyDrill: For one decision today: write best case, worst case, and most likely case. Then decide.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: run scenario analysis on 3 decisions before making them.

basic.name: Risk assessment
basic.dailyDrill: Identify one risk in your current project. Rate it: likelihood (1–5) × impact (1–5). Write a mitigation.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: identify and mitigate 3 risks before they materialize.

basic.name: Cost-benefit analysis
basic.dailyDrill: Pick one tradeoff you made today. Write the cost and benefit in one line each. Was it right?
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: make 3 decisions using explicit cost-benefit analysis.

basic.name: Decisiveness under pressure
basic.dailyDrill: Make one decision you've been postponing. Set a 5-minute timer. Decide before it rings.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: make 5 decisions you would normally have deferred.

basic.name: Intuition calibration
basic.dailyDrill: Write one gut feeling you had today. Was it right? Write the data that supports or refutes it.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: track 5 gut feelings. Measure your calibration at week end.

basic.name: Contingency planning
basic.dailyDrill: For your most important task this week: write what you do if it fails completely.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: have a contingency plan for your top 3 risks.

basic.name: Negotiation
basic.dailyDrill: In one interaction today: anchor first with your preferred outcome. Then listen — don't fill the silence.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: use anchoring in 3 real interactions. Note the result each time.

basic.name: Persuasion techniques
basic.dailyDrill: Write one thing you want someone to agree with. Frame it starting with their benefit, not yours.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: persuade someone of something using their values, not yours.

basic.name: Concession crafting
basic.dailyDrill: Identify one thing you can offer as a concession that costs you little but matters to them.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: make 2 strategic concessions that strengthened your position.

basic.name: BATNA development
basic.dailyDrill: In one context where you want something: write your best alternative if you don't get it.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: define your BATNA for your top 2 ongoing negotiations.

basic.name: Closing & commitment
basic.dailyDrill: Ask for a clear yes/no on one thing today. Politely refuse to accept 'I'll think about it'.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: close 3 open loops with a concrete commitment from another person.

basic.name: Networking
basic.dailyDrill: Send one genuine, value-first message to someone in your field. No ask attached.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: make 3 new meaningful connections. One must be senior to you.

basic.name: Elevator-pitch mastery
basic.dailyDrill: Write a 3-sentence pitch for yourself: who you are, what you build, your unique edge. Say it out loud.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: refine your elevator pitch until it sounds completely natural.

basic.name: Value-first outreach
basic.dailyDrill: Before reaching out to anyone today: write what value you will give them first.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: get one positive response from a cold outreach that led with value.

basic.name: Follow-up discipline
basic.dailyDrill: Check your open threads. Send one follow-up that's been sitting for 3+ days.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: close every open loop. No dangling threads.

basic.name: Strategic introductions
basic.dailyDrill: Think of two people who should know each other. Write the connection reason in one sentence.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: make one strategic introduction. Get a thank-you from both sides.

basic.name: Relationship nurturing
basic.dailyDrill: Reconnect with one person you haven't spoken to in 3+ weeks. No agenda. Just check in.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: strengthen 2 existing relationships with deliberate attention.

basic.name: Time Management
basic.dailyDrill: Write your top 3 outcomes for tomorrow before you sleep. Nothing else counts.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: achieve your top 3 outcomes 5 out of 5 days.

basic.name: Prioritization (Eisenhower Matrix)
basic.dailyDrill: List today's tasks. Assign each to one quadrant: Urgent+Important, Important+Not urgent, Urgent+Not Important, Neither.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: eliminate 3 quadrant-4 tasks permanently from your list.

basic.name: Task batching & deep-work
basic.dailyDrill: Group 3 similar tasks and do them in one unbroken block. No context switching.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: complete 3 deep-work blocks of 90+ minutes each.

basic.name: Scheduling with buffers
basic.dailyDrill: Add a 20% time buffer to one task you estimate today. Track whether it was needed.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: estimate 5 tasks with buffers. Measure actual vs estimated.

basic.name: Deadline adherence
basic.dailyDrill: Name one deadline this week. Write the exact date and time. Treat it as a flight departure.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: meet every deadline you set. Renegotiate early if needed — never miss silently.

basic.name: Personal Branding
basic.dailyDrill: Write one sentence: what do you want to be known for in your industry in 2 years?
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: align one visible action (post, project, message) to your brand.

basic.name: Professional attire & grooming
basic.dailyDrill: Before any important call or meeting: check mirror. Is this how you want to be seen?
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: show up intentionally dressed for every important interaction.

basic.name: Confident posture
basic.dailyDrill: Set a timer for 5 minutes. Sit upright, shoulders back, feet flat. Hold it for the full time.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: hold confident posture as your default for all video calls.

basic.name: Signature introduction
basic.dailyDrill: Refine your name + role + one sentence value prop to 10 seconds. Say it out loud. Time it.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: your introduction is smooth, natural, and memorable every time.

basic.name: Online presence
basic.dailyDrill: Review your LinkedIn or GitHub profile. Write one specific thing to update or add this week.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: your online presence reflects your current level. No outdated info.

basic.name: Thought-leadership content
basic.dailyDrill: Write one insight from today's work that could genuinely help another developer.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: publish or share one piece of technical content publicly.

basic.name: Learning Agility
basic.dailyDrill: Name one thing you were wrong about this week. Write what changed your mind.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: change your mind on something based on evidence. Write what it was.

basic.name: Metacognitive reflection
basic.dailyDrill: After your study session: write what you actually learned vs what you thought you'd learn.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: identify one systematic gap in your learning process and fix it.

basic.name: Rapid skill acquisition
basic.dailyDrill: Pick one micro-skill. Practice it with deliberate repetition for 20 minutes. Log your improvement.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: acquire one new micro-skill to functional level from zero.

basic.name: Feedback solicitation & integration
basic.dailyDrill: Ask one person for specific feedback on something you produced today. Make it easy for them to be honest.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: implement one piece of feedback you received. Tell the giver what changed.

basic.name: Experimentation mindset
basic.dailyDrill: Run one small experiment today. Write your hypothesis before you start. Log the result.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: run 3 micro-experiments. At least one should disprove your assumption.

basic.name: Unlearning bad habits
basic.dailyDrill: Name one habit that's slowing you down. Write one concrete replacement behavior to try tomorrow.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: replace one bad habit with a better one. Verify with 5 days of evidence.

basic.name: Divergent thinking
basic.dailyDrill: Name one problem. Write 10 possible solutions in 5 minutes — no filtering, no judgment.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: generate 10 ideas per day for 5 days. Pick the best one each day.

basic.name: Idea synthesis
basic.dailyDrill: Combine one thing you learned today with something from a completely different field. Write the mashup.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: create 3 cross-domain idea mashups. Share the best one.

basic.name: Conceptual blending
basic.dailyDrill: Take two unrelated concepts from today's study. Write one way they secretly overlap.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: find 5 unexpected conceptual connections across different topics.

basic.name: Rapid prototyping
basic.dailyDrill: Build the simplest possible version of one idea today. 30 minutes max. Ship it or sketch it.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: build 3 rough prototypes. Done beats perfect.

basic.name: "Yes, and…" improvisation
basic.dailyDrill: In one conversation today: instead of 'but', say 'yes, and'. Notice how the energy shifts.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: build on someone else's idea 5 times without shooting it down first.

basic.name: Resilience & Stress Management
basic.dailyDrill: Rate your stress today 1–10. Write one cause and one concrete action to reduce it.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: bring your average daily stress below 6 through deliberate action.

basic.name: Stress-response recognition
basic.dailyDrill: The next time you feel stress: name it, locate it in your body, breathe in 4 counts, out 4 counts.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: catch your stress response early 5 times. Log each one.

basic.name: Recovery rituals (sleep, meditation)
basic.dailyDrill: Write your sleep time from last night. Was it 7+ hours? Write one thing that blocked good sleep.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: hit 7+ hours of sleep for 5 consecutive nights.

basic.name: Adaptability to change
basic.dailyDrill: Name one thing that didn't go as planned today. Write what you did differently as a result.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: adapt your approach 3 times mid-task without complaining.

basic.name: Persistence through setbacks
basic.dailyDrill: Name one thing you gave up on too quickly recently. Write the next smallest step forward.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: keep going on one thing you'd normally quit. Log every moment of resistance.

basic.name: Growth-mindset reinforcement
basic.dailyDrill: Write 'I can't do X yet' for one skill gap. Add the word 'yet' and notice how it changes the feeling.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: catch yourself with a fixed mindset 3 times. Reframe each one.

basic.name: Influence & Persuasion
basic.dailyDrill: Identify one outcome you want from someone. Write the narrative that aligns with their values.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: influence one outcome using their motivations — not yours.

basic.name: Storytelling framework
basic.dailyDrill: Tell one technical story today using: situation → complication → resolution. No jargon.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: tell 5 stories using the SCR framework. Get feedback on one.

basic.name: Social-proof deployment
basic.dailyDrill: In one message or pitch today: reference one real person or company that succeeded with this approach.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: build a file of 5 social proof examples you can use in pitches.

basic.name: Authority signaling
basic.dailyDrill: Share one insight today backed by a credible source. Cite it. Explain why it matters.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: build credibility in 3 interactions through demonstrated expertise.

basic.name: Reciprocity leverage
basic.dailyDrill: Give something today — help, a resource, a referral — with zero expectation attached.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: give 5 things without expecting anything back. Track what comes back anyway.

basic.name: Scarcity & urgency cues
basic.dailyDrill: In one message today: add a specific deadline or constraint. Vague urgency gets ignored.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: add a real constraint to 3 requests. Measure response rate.

basic.name: Public Speaking
basic.dailyDrill: Explain today's toughest concept out loud to an imaginary audience for 2 minutes. No notes.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: give one 5-minute verbal explanation without notes to a real person.

basic.name: Stage presence & poise
basic.dailyDrill: Before your next video call: stand for 2 minutes with good posture. Then sit. Notice the difference.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: command attention in 3 calls or presentations through physical presence.

basic.name: Vocal variety & pacing
basic.dailyDrill: Record yourself reading one technical paragraph. Listen for monotone. Re-record with variation.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: vary your pace and emphasis in 5 technical explanations.

basic.name: Audience engagement
basic.dailyDrill: In your next technical explanation: ask one question to the listener mid-way through.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: keep 3 different listeners engaged for 5+ minutes each.

basic.name: Structured messaging (opening, body, close)
basic.dailyDrill: Write your next email with: 1-sentence context → 2–3 sentences body → 1 clear ask. Nothing else.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: use this structure for every email and message you send.

basic.name: Handling Q&A with confidence
basic.dailyDrill: Practice answering one question you don't fully know: 'Great question. Here's how I'd think through it...'
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: handle 3 unexpected questions without saying 'I don't know' as a full answer.

basic.name: Writing & Documentation
basic.dailyDrill: Rewrite one comment, README section, or message you wrote today. Make it shorter and clearer.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: produce one piece of documentation someone else calls 'actually useful'.

basic.name: Clarity and brevity
basic.dailyDrill: Cut the word count of one thing you wrote today by 30%. The meaning must survive.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: write 5 messages that say exactly what's needed — nothing more.

basic.name: Structured narrative (Pyramid Principle)
basic.dailyDrill: Rewrite one explanation: lead with the conclusion, then give supporting points.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: use bottom-line-first structure in 3 written communications.

basic.name: Tone-matching for audience
basic.dailyDrill: Rewrite one technical message for a non-technical stakeholder. Same information, different language.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: adapt your writing tone to 3 different audiences correctly.

basic.name: Concise email etiquette
basic.dailyDrill: Write one email response in 5 sentences or fewer. Include one specific action item.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: every email you send is under 100 words unless absolutely necessary.

basic.name: Argument scaffolding
basic.dailyDrill: Write one argument with: claim → evidence → implication. Three parts. Nothing else.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: make 5 arguments using this structure. Get agreement on 3 of them.

basic.name: Analytical & Quantitative Skills
basic.dailyDrill: Find one metric in your project today. Write what it means and whether it's moving the right way.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: define 3 metrics that actually tell you if your project is healthy.

basic.name: Data interpretation
basic.dailyDrill: Look at one chart or number from today. Write two things it does NOT tell you.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: correctly interpret 5 datasets without drawing unsupported conclusions.

basic.name: Basic statistics (mean, variance)
basic.dailyDrill: Calculate the mean and standard deviation of 5 numbers from your work today. Interpret the variance.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: use basic stats to make one real decision.

basic.name: Spreadsheet modeling
basic.dailyDrill: Build a 5-row spreadsheet model for one decision you're making. Use at least one formula.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: build a model that helped you make one real decision.

basic.name: Trend-spotting
basic.dailyDrill: Look at your last 7 days of progress. Write one clear trend — positive or negative.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: identify 3 trends in your work or learning. Act on one.

basic.name: Hypothesis testing
basic.dailyDrill: Write one testable hypothesis about your code or learning. Write exactly how you'd disprove it.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: run 3 hypothesis tests. Be willing to be wrong.

basic.name: Financial Literacy
basic.dailyDrill: Review last week's spending in 5 minutes. Name one thing to cut and one thing worth more.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: understand exactly where your money went last month.

basic.name: Budget creation
basic.dailyDrill: Write a simple 3-category budget for this month: needs / wants / savings. Assign percentages.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: have a written budget you can actually stick to.

basic.name: Cash-flow forecasting
basic.dailyDrill: Write your expected income and fixed expenses for next month. What's the gap?
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: forecast your cash flow for the next 3 months.

basic.name: ROI calculation
basic.dailyDrill: Calculate the ROI of one tool or course you've paid for: value gained divided by money spent.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: audit every recurring subscription for ROI. Cut one.

basic.name: Break-even analysis
basic.dailyDrill: For one project or side hustle idea: calculate the break-even point in units, days, or hours.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: know the break-even for any project you're investing time or money in.

basic.name: Basic accounting principles
basic.dailyDrill: Write the difference between an asset and a liability using one example from your life.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: classify your 5 largest possessions as assets or liabilities.

basic.name: Cultural & Social Intelligence
basic.dailyDrill: In one interaction today: notice one cultural assumption you made. Write it down honestly.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: identify 5 cultural assumptions you hold. Verify 2 of them.

basic.name: Cross-cultural awareness
basic.dailyDrill: Read one 5-minute piece about communication norms in a culture different from yours.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: adjust your communication style once based on cross-cultural awareness.

basic.name: Language etiquette
basic.dailyDrill: Review one message you sent today. Was your tone appropriate for that person's context and culture?
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: send 5 messages where you consciously adapted language to the recipient.

basic.name: Norm-reading in new contexts
basic.dailyDrill: Think of the last new environment you entered. Write 3 unspoken rules you had to figure out.
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: successfully navigate one new social or professional context.

basic.name: Inclusive communication
basic.dailyDrill: Review one thing you wrote today. Is it accessible to someone outside your field or background?
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: rewrite 3 pieces of communication to be more inclusive.

basic.name: Adaptive behavior
basic.dailyDrill: Name one situation today where you adjusted your approach mid-stream. What prompted it?
basic.weeklyGoal: By end of week: adapt your behavior in 3 situations based on reading the room.
